content: Use compose override files (docker-compose.production.yml) for production-specific config like pinned images — never modify the base compose files that developers use daily
After changing compose structure (build: to image:, adding overrides), verify container names with docker compose ps — compose may generate unexpected prefixed names
When enabling TLS on any database, grep ALL connection strings including test fixtures and CI scripts — hardcoded sslmode=disable in tests bypasses encryption silently
Create a small housekeeping bead to commit accumulated pending changes (Cargo.lock, proof artifacts, infra files) rather than letting uncommitted changes pile up across agents
Structure security hardening as independent beads touching separate file sets (Dockerfiles, compose-data, compose-services) to enable parallel agent execution without file conflicts
Always set explicit container_name in compose services to prevent naming drift when switching between build: and image: directives or adding compose overrides
When pinning production images, create a release manifest script that generates version env files from git tags — enables reproducible deploys and rollback by changing one env file
